question_text: What is the elastic limit of a spring?

options.label: A
options.text: The maximum force that can ever be applied to a spring

options.label: B
options.text: The point beyond which a spring will not return to its original shape

options.label: C
options.text: The point where the spring breaks into pieces

options.label: D
options.text: The force needed to stretch a spring by 1 centimetre

correct_answer: The point beyond which a spring will not return to its original shape
explanation: The elastic limit is the maximum force that can be applied to a spring while still allowing it to return to its original length and shape. If you exceed this limit, the spring becomes permanently deformed and won't bounce back. This is an important concept when studying springs and forces.
